title: Getting Started
summary: Complete setup guide for installing and running the documentation generation system

blocks.text: This guide will help you set up and run the documentation generation system to automatically create comprehensive documentation for your codebase using LLM-powered agents.

items.text: **OpenAI API key** or **Yandex GPT API key**
Access to your codebase for documentation generation

blocks.text: First, clone the repository and install dependencies:

blocks.code:
# Install dependencies using uv
uv sync

blocks.text: Start the Qdrant vector database using Docker:
Copy the example configuration file and set up your API keys:

blocks.code:
# Copy example configuration
cp config.example.yaml config.yaml

blocks.text: Set your OpenAI API key as an environment variable:
For Yandex GPT, modify the configuration:

blocks.code:
llm:
  provider: yandex
  model: yandexgpt
  api_key: ${YANDEX_API_KEY}
  base_url: https://llm.api.cloud.yandex.net/v1

blocks.text: Generate documentation for your codebase with the following command:

blocks.code: uv run python -m docgen_core.cli generate /path/to/codebase --config my_config.yaml --output docs.json

blocks.text: The system follows an event-driven pipeline:

items.text: **Ingestion**: Your codebase is analyzed and indexed
**Vector Storage**: Code embeddings are stored in Qdrant
**Planning**: AI agent plans the documentation structure
**Output**: Documentation is saved as structured JSON
**snapshot_hash**: Hash of the codebase for tracking changes
**nodes**: List of documentation pages with content blocks
**citations**: References to the original source code

blocks.text: Each documentation page contains various content blocks (headings, paragraphs, code examples) with proper citations to the source code.

items.text: **Qdrant connection error**: Ensure Docker container is running on port 6333
**API key issues**: Verify environment variables are set correctly
**Memory errors**: Consider using smaller codebases or increasing system resources
**Generation timeout**: Check network connectivity and API rate limits

blocks.text: Use the `--clear-qdrant` flag if you need to reset the vector database:
The system provides detailed logging to help diagnose issues. Check the console output for generation progress and any error messages.
